title: SpeedTrader Tips and Tricks Every Trader Should Know (2026)

meta_description: SpeedTrader Tips and Tricks Every Trader Should Know (2026). SpeedTrader (4.1★, Free). Pro tips, hidden features, and common mistakes to avoid. Updated 2026.
excerpt: Insider tips and tricks for SpeedTrader that most traders never discover. Level up your workflow.
content:
Why SpeedTrader Tips Matter

SpeedTrader's Direct Market Access (DMA) execution engine, multiple exchange routing options, and professional-grade tools give you an edge—but only if you configure them correctly. Most traders leave money on the table by using default settings, missing routing optimization, or failing to leverage SpeedTrader's short locate integration. This guide covers the 80% of SpeedTrader's features most day traders never discover, from micro-optimizations that shave milliseconds off order execution to risk management workflows that prevent costly mistakes.

Setup Tips

1. Configure Your Exchange Routing Priority Based on Market Conditions

SpeedTrader lets you choose between NASDAQ, NYSE, ARCA, BATS/EDGX, and ECN routing. Don't set it and forget it. In Account Settings → Routing Preferences, create separate profiles for morning market open (use ARCA for liquidity), mid-day scalping (switch to NASDAQ for tighter spreads), and pre-close trading (use multiple routing to hit the best bid/ask).

Pro move: Use BATS/EDGX routing for low-volume stocks where you want minimal market impact. The exchange's order-handling rules are looser, giving you more flexibility on route selection.

2. Link Your Charting Tool First, Before You Start Trading

SpeedTrader's built-in charting is execution-focused, not analysis-focused. Before your first trading day, integrate a third-party tool—most traders use ThinkorSwim, eSignal, or Thinkorswim's paper trading mirror. Under Settings → Platform Integration, set your default charting tool. This eliminates the friction of alt-tabbing during trades and ensures your price data stays synchronized with your execution terminal.

3. Customize Your Level 2 Window Layout for Your Patterns

Your Level 2 display should reflect your edge. If you trade momentum off the open, resize your window to show the top 10 bid/ask levels and order flow intensity. If you're a scalper playing support/resistance, emphasize time-of-sale and VWAP. Go to View → Level 2 Customization → Advanced Layout, and save multiple profiles (Morning Profile, Afternoon Profile). Switching profiles takes one click—your eyes adjust faster than recreating the layout each session.

4. Set Up Hotkeys for Your Most-Used Orders Before Market Open

SpeedTrader supports custom hotkey macros. Go to Tools → Hotkey Manager and program instant-execution hotkeys for: buy 500 shares at bid, sell 500 at ask, buy half-size, add to position, and exit. Map these to F1, F2, F3, F4, F5. During fast markets, clicking buttons costs you 100-200ms. Hotkeys cut that to 10ms. Practice muscle memory during paper trading.

Trading Tips

1. Use Level 2 Tape Reading to Detect Fake Support Before Your Entry

SpeedTrader's Level 2 data shows order flow, but most traders only watch price. Watch the bid stack: if a 10,000-share bid appears, drops, then reappears 20 cents lower, it's a spoofing pattern. Your order will fill, then the bid evaporates. Instead, enter after you see 3+ consecutive prints at the same level without the bid collapsing. This single rule cuts losing trades by 20%.

2. Route Small Orders to ECNs for Rebates, Large Orders to ARCA for Liquidity

SpeedTrader's commission structure charges differently per exchange. Small 100-200 share orders routed to ECNs (NASDAQ, Inet) often qualify for liquidity rebates—you pay $0.00 instead of per-share fees. Large 500+ share orders go to ARCA, which absorbs your entire order without routing fragmentation. Under Order Ticket → Routing, you can override the default route per order. Make this habit: ask yourself "is this small and narrow or large and wide?" before hitting submit.

3. Leverage Short Locate Integration to Avoid Fails and Forced Exits

SpeedTrader integrates directly with share lending. Before shorting, check Tools → Short Locate → Borrow Availability. Filter by commission rate (go for sub-1% lenders) and availability duration (24-hour borrows are risky). Your short order only executes if locate is available—never place a short without confirming borrow status. This prevents the nightmare of being forced to buy back at market after a failed short.

4. Use Pre-Market Level 2 to Identify Overnight News Momentum

Most brokers don't let you see pre-market Level 2 clearly. SpeedTrader does (4 a.m. ET starts). Watch 5:45-6:15 a.m. for institutions moving size after earnings or news. If you see 100k+ shares printed at prices away from the previous close, the stock is gapping. Prepare your morning playbook by 6:30 a.m., not during the chaos. Your edge is prep work, not reaction time.

5. Set Profit Targets as Trailing Stops Instead of Static Limits

Under Order Ticket → Advanced → Profit Target, use Trailing Stop % instead of fixed-price targets. A 2% trailing stop on a scalp trade locks in gains while letting winners run. If you sell on a fixed target, you miss 40% of your upside on the days your edge is strongest. This is psychology: let the market prove you wrong instead of capping your winners.

6. Monitor Your Fill Quality via Order Statistics (Not Just Win Rate)

Go to Reports → Fill Analysis weekly. Most traders only check P&L. Check: average slippage per trade, execution speed by time-of-day, and route effectiveness. If you notice 50% of your limit orders miss fills on ARCA but hit 90% on NASDAQ, you're using the wrong default route. One percentage point of better fill quality = $2,000+ per month on 50-trade days.

Risk Management Tips

1. Use Position Size Rules, Not Mental Stops

SpeedTrader's Risk Manager lets you set hard position limits. Before market open, go to Account → Risk Controls → Position Sizing and set a max position based on your account size and R:R (e.g., max 500 shares if your stop is 5 cents wide = $25 risk). The platform will reject orders that exceed this. This removes the temptation to pyramid into winners—your risk is fixed, not your winner size.

2. Enable Daily Loss Limits to Prevent Emotional Escalation

Set Account Settings → Daily Loss Limit → Hard Stop at -$500 (or your number). Once you hit it, SpeedTrader closes all positions and locks new orders until tomorrow. This sounds harsh but prevents the scenario where one bad trade turns into three worse ones because you're trying to "get even." Pros know the day is over; beginners revenge trade.

3. Track Slippage by Session and Adjust Your Timing Strategy

Under Reports → Execution Stats, compare your average slippage during 9:30-10 a.m. (chaotic), 10-11 a.m. (normalizing), and last hour (algorithmic). Most traders lose money early and evening—they're fighting volatility. If your 11 a.m.–2 p.m. slippage is 60% better, concentrate your volume there. Stop forcing trades into bad market conditions.

4. Use Alerts to Monitor Open Positions Instead of Staring

Set price-based alerts (not notifications—actual alerts that stop trading). Go to Tools → Alerts → Position Monitoring and set: "Alert if position hits +$500 (take profit target)" and "Alert if position hits -$150 (stop loss)." Alerts pause new order entry until you acknowledge. This forces you to be intentional about your exit, not mindless about holding.

Advanced Tips

1. Access SpeedTrader's API for Custom Order Automation

SpeedTrader exposes a REST API (documentation under Developer Portal) for automated entry/exit. If you have a repeatable pattern (e.g., "enter on level 2 uptick, exit on lower high"), you can write a 50-line Python script that monitors Level 2 and submits orders faster than your fingers can. This isn't for beginners—it requires testing—but it turns a manual 3-second process into a 200ms automation.

2. Route Orders Based on Predicted Spread Using Order Flow

Advanced technique: before submitting a limit order, check the Bid/Ask Spread Volatility Meter (under Level 2 → Analytics). If the spread widens to 2+ cents, route to ARCA (guaranteed fill). If the spread is tight (1 cent or less), route to ECNs (rebate likely). This adds 100ms of decision time but increases fill rates by 15%.

3. Use Sterling Trader Pro Instead of SpeedTrader PRO for Extra Latency Control

Many pros switch from SpeedTrader's native terminal to Sterling Trader Pro (a third-party platform SpeedTrader allows). Sterling gives you sub-microsecond order submission tuning and direct route control down to individual ECN servers. If you're scalping with 50+ trades/day, Sterling's API is worth the learning curve. Compare: SpeedTrader PRO is fast for most traders; Sterling is fast for obsessives.

4. Monitor Your Rebate Accrual and Reinvest It Into Your Platform

SpeedTrader's billing dashboard (Account → Billing → Rebates) shows exactly how much you've earned from ECN rebates that month. Most traders ignore this. If you're earning $200/month in rebates, that's platform fees essentially free. Once rebates exceed your trading costs, consider upgrading to DAS Trader Pro (SpeedTrader's pro terminal) for better charting and analytics—you're already profitable on commissions.

5. Build a "Session Playbook" Template and Reuse It

Create a text file (Google Docs works) with your game plan: "If QQQ opens +2% and prints above the 20-min VWAP, enter 300 shares on dips to VWAP. Scale out 50% at +0.20, trail stop +0.10 on remainder." Copy this same playbook daily and mark up your results. After 20 days of repetition, your hands automate the routine. Muscle memory beats decision fatigue in day trading.

Common Mistakes to Avoid

1. Mistake: Using Default NASDAQ Routing for All Stocks

Why it costs: NASDAQ is fast but charges higher ECN fees. Small caps trade better on regional exchanges. Fix: Check Stock → Details → Primary Exchange. If it's listed on NYSE but primarily trades on ARCA, route there. This single change cuts commissions by $10-15 per 50-trade day.

2. Mistake: Not Confirming Short Locate Before Hitting Sell

Why it costs: SpeedTrader will reject your short order if borrow isn't available, but by then you've mentally committed to the trade and may revenge-buy. Fix: Always check Tools → Short Locate 5 minutes before your entry. If commission is >1%, skip the short—it's not worth the squeeze risk.

3. Mistake: Setting Profit Targets Too Tight on Your Best Patterns

Why it costs: Your 0.10 profit target hits, you exit 100 shares, then the stock moves 0.30 more without you. You've capped your winners. Fix: Use Trailing Stop % (not fixed price targets) on your highest-conviction setups. Lock base profit at 1%, let the rest trail at 3% stop.

4. Mistake: Ignoring Slippage Creep

Why it costs: Your average slippage drifts from 1 cent to 2 cents per trade, costing $500/month unnoticed. Fix: Check Reports → Execution Stats every Friday. If slippage exceeds your baseline, audit your routing settings. The platform shows you exactly which routes underperform.

5. Mistake: Holding Overnight Without a Plan

Why it costs: SpeedTrader's commission structure and DMA routing optimize for day trades. Holding overnight exposes you to gap risk, and you lose your execution edge in pre-market. Fix: Close 100% of positions by 3:50 p.m. If your trade isn't ready to close, it's not ready to hold overnight.

SpeedTrader vs. Alternatives: When to Switch

SpeedTrader dominates for sub-5-second hold trades with sub-1,000 share orders on US equities. But if you're trading options (limited routing control on SpeedTrader), futures (not supported), or want integrated charting (Ninjatrader, Thinkorswim are better), you need a different tool. Check our SpeedTrader vs. Tradovate and SpeedTrader vs. thinkorSwim comparisons to see if a competitor fits your edge better. For pure equities DMA, SpeedTrader is our top-rated broker—these tips will unlock its full potential.
author: TradingToolsHub Editorial
